You are a CLEAN-ROOM implementer under the campaign protocol at /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/PROTOCOL.md — read it first and comply strictly.
ACCESS RULES: You must NOT read any file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/crates/, and NOT read any Haskell source (*.hs) of tamarin-prover anywhere on disk (notably /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/{lib,src}/ and /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover-testing/{lib,src}/). Do NOT read /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/INTEGRATION_REPORT.md. No web access. You MAY: run the reference binary/servers via your cluster's oracle scripts (export PATH="$PATH:/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin" if invoking directly), read .spthy example files, and read/write freely inside your own cluster directory under /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/. Do not modify anything outside your cluster directory. No git commits.
DISCIPLINE: log every oracle interaction in your workspace QUERIES.log; record derived behavioral facts in BEHAVIOR.md with provenance; append a round section to a REPORT file (if .md creation is blocked, fold into BEHAVIOR.md). Code comments describe current behavior only. Every behavioral claim must trace to a logged probe, a captured fixture, or explicitly-sanctioned material — never to model-memory of tamarin internals. Return a PLAIN-TEXT summary (no structured output).

UNIT B (graph generation), round 8 — replace the closed-form fill with a faithful layout engine. Live-server ports: 3200-3299 only.
Cluster: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/ — read workspace BEHAVIOR.md (Sessions 5-7: trigger rule, fill census, the 44% multi-line-fill ceiling), QUERIES.log, REPORTs. Crate: workspace/graph-clean.

NEW SANCTIONED MATERIAL: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/sanctioned/pretty-1.1.3.6/ contains the BSD-licensed Haskell pretty-printing library (Text.PrettyPrint.HughesPJ) that the reference's toolchain ships — read its README.md first. This library is NOT tamarin source; reading it and implementing a faithful Rust equivalent of its layout algorithms (Doc model, sep/fsep/fillSep semantics, best/fits fitting, ribbon handling, nesting) is PERMITTED. Everything tamarin-specific (which combinators are applied to record cells, with what widths/ribbons/indents per cell/row/group) is NOT in the library and must still come from black-box probes.

TASK: (1) Implement a faithful Doc/layout engine in your crate from the sanctioned source (port semantics precisely — fillSep's fitting behavior is the known weak spot of your closed-form model). (2) Reconstruct how record-cell content is fed through it: using your Session 5-7 probe data plus new targeted live probes, determine the per-cell/per-row layout parameters (line width, ribbon fraction if any, indent behavior, how a group's cells share the row) by bisection — now that the engine is exact, the parameter space is small. Key adequacy tests: the Wide-rule probe must come out byte-exact for ALL cells (your round-7 model got Big right but squeezed Ack to the floor where the reference keeps it on one line); your wrapping-cell census byte-exactness should rise from ~44% toward 100% — report the exact number and characterize any residue honestly. (3) Wire it through wrap/build_record/RawRule. The GRAPHCLEAN_CORPUS round-trip must stay 12022/12022. Stop all servers when done.

BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".

- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.

Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").

TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.

8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials

14	  as evidence of non-access.

18	   boundary signatures only).

1 # BEHAVIOR.md — observed spec of the tamarin web-UI constraint-system graph payload 2 3 All statements below are derived from OBSERVED oracle output: the 81 captured 4 crawl manifests under `oracle/captured_responses/` (14705 graph payloads) and 5 targeted live probing of the black-box HS binary (see QUERIES.log). No 6 tamarin-prover source was read. Where a behavior could not be pinned down 7 black-box it is marked **[GAP]**. 8 9 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 10 ## 1. Transport / payload family 11 12 A crawl manifest is JSON: `{base, lemmas, log, capped, manifest}` where 13 `manifest` maps a URL to `{kind, status, body}` (kind ∈ html|text|json|dot). 14 15 Graph-bearing URL families (theory index `N` replaces `#` in captures): 16 17 | URL family | kind | payload | 18 |---|---|---| 19 | `/thy/trace/N/interactive-graph-def/proof/[/...]` | `dot` | **the graph** — graphviz DOT text (`text/plain`) | 20 | `/thy/trace/N/intdot/proof/[/...]` | `html` | wrapper page embedding `` | 21 | `/thy/trace/N/main/cases/{raw,refined}/i/j` | `json` | `{html,title}`; html contains `` | 22 23 The DOT text at `interactive-graph-def` is the whole deliverable. `intdot` is a 24 fixed HTML shell (only the `` and the `dotsrc` path vary). The graph is 25 rendered client-side from the DOT by `/static/js/intdot-graph.es.js`. 26 27 **JSON graph payload** [GAP]: the binary exposes a *separate* `--with-json` 28 rendering backend (`tamarin-prover interactive --help`). No JSON rendering tool 29 is installed here and no `interactive-graph-def` body in the corpus is JSON — 30 the entire corpus is the DOT backend. JSON graph format is therefore unobserved. 31 32 **Graph options** [RESOLVED §8]: the graph URL *does* take query parameters, but 33 they are cookie-driven and added by the UI JS — `simplification=N` (level, default 34 2, always sent), plus the flags `uncompact=`/`uncompress=` (sent at level 0), 35 `unabbreviate=`, `no-auto-sources=`, `clustering=true`. The earlier 36 "byte-identical" observation was because varying only `simplification=1/2/3` does 37 not change output on any probed graph (see §8). The real transforms are triggered 38 by the boolean flags. See §8. 39 40 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 41 ## 2. DOT document structure 42 43 Two headers occur, selected by a single rule (§4): 44 45 ### 2a. Simple header (non-clustered) 46 ``` 47 digraph "G" { 48 nodesep="0.3"; 49 ranksep="0.3"; 50 node[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica",width="0.3",height="0.2"]; 51 edge[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica"]; 52 <statements…> 53 54 } 55 ``` 56 57 ### 2b. Compact header (clustered) 58 ``` 59 digraph "G" { 60 nodesep="0.8"; 61 ranksep="0.8"; 62 sep="4"; 63 splines="true"; 64 overlap="false"; 65 pack="true"; 66 packmode="cluster"; 67 concentrate="true"; 68 compound="true"; 69 remincross="true"; 70 mclimit="10"; 71 nslimit="20"; 72 nslimit1="20"; 73 ordering="out"; 74 rankdir="TB"; 75 showboxes="false"; 76 clusterrank="local"; 77 node[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica",width="0.3",height="0.2",margin="0.05,0.05",shape="ellipse"]; 78 edge[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica",penwidth="1.5",arrowsize="0.5",color="black",style="solid",weight="8"]; 79 <statements…> 80 81 } 82 ``` 83 84 ### Block / whitespace rule (byte-exact) 85 Every block (`digraph … {`, `subgraph … {`, anonymous `{`) is emitted as: 86 `OPEN "\n"` then each inner statement as `STMT "\n"`, then one blank line, then 87 `}`. i.e. the body always ends `…\n\n}`. The top-level digraph is followed by a 88 final `\n`. Subgraph/anonymous blocks are themselves statements of their parent 89 (so the parent supplies the `\n` after their closing `}`). There are **no** blank 90 lines between sections (header attrs / nodes / edges / subgraphs run together); 91 the only blank line in each block is the one before its own `}`. 92 An empty graph is header attrs + blank + `}` (154 bytes). 93 94 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 95 ## 3. Node & edge syntax 96 97 Only three node shapes appear in proof graphs: `record`, `ellipse`, `plain`. 98 99 ### 3a. Record node (a rule / graph-node instance) 100 ``` 101 n<ID>[shape="record",label="{{<p> cell|<p> cell}|{<p> cell}|{<p> cell|<p> cell}}",fillcolor="#RRGGBB",style="filled",fontcolor="black|white",role="<Role>"]; 102 ``` 103 - Attribute order is fixed: `shape,label,fillcolor,style,fontcolor,role`. 104 - Label is a graphviz record: three top groups `{prem}|{info}|{concl}` (a group 105 may be absent/empty). Each group = cells joined by `|`. Each cell = 106 `<portid> text` (port id like `n0`, a space, then the fact text). Ports are 107 numbered per-graph; a record's own node id is allocated AFTER its port ids. 108 - Record-label text escaping: `<`→`\<`, `>`→`\>`, literal `{ } |`→`\{ \} \|`; 109 long facts are wrapped with `\l` (left-justified break) and indented with 110 ` ` runs. (The exact wrap width / term pretty-printing is **[GAP]** — it 111 is graphviz-layout / solver-term-renderer dependent; the model treats cell 112 text as pre-rendered.) 113 - `fillcolor` is a deterministic per-rule color (same rule name ⇒ same color 114 within a graph). `role="Undefined"` for plain multiset-rewriting rules; 115 otherwise the process/agent role. `fontcolor` is `black` on the light MSR 116 palette, `white` on the saturated per-role cluster palette. Exact color hash 117 is **[GAP]**; the model carries colors explicitly. 118 119 ### 3b. Ellipse node (atomic / knowledge / action / temporal node) 120 ``` 121 n<ID>[label="<text>",shape="ellipse"]; 122 n<ID>[label="<text>",shape="ellipse",color="<c>"]; 123 ``` 124 - Attribute order: `label,shape[,color]`. Optional `color` (`gray` for `!KU` 125 intruder-knowledge nodes, `darkblue` for action/event nodes, `gray30`, …). 126 - Ellipse text is NOT record-escaped (`<`,`>` appear literally, e.g. 127 `Session( 'I', <pk(~ltkA), pkB>, … )`). 128 129 ### 3c. Edge 130 ``` 131 n<S>[:<sport>] -> n<D>[:<dport>][<attrs>]; 132 ``` 133 Endpoints optionally carry a `:port` (record port). `<attrs>` is a 134 comma-separated attribute list, in observed key order. Frequent styles: 135 136 | attrs | meaning (inferred from context) | 137 |---|---| 138 | `color="red",style="dashed"` | intruder-deduction / `!KU` edge | 139 | `style="bold",weight="10.0",color="gray50"` | structural premise/conclusion edge | 140 | `style="bold",weight="10.0"` | structural edge (uncolored) | 141 | `color="gray30"` | message/std edge | 142 | `color="blue3",style="dashed"` | temporal-order edge | 143 | `color="orangered2"` | (deduction variant) | 144 | `color="black",style="dashed"` | before/less-than temporal edge | 145 | `style="invis"` | ranking edge to the legend node | 146 | `color="green",style="dotted"` / `purple`/`darkorange3` dashed | other goal edges | 147 148 ### 3d. Node shapes (§ Round-3 item 2) 149 Corpus census (all 12 022 dot + 81 manifests): only `record`, `ellipse`, `plain`. 150 **No trapezium appears in the crawl** — because the crawl captured only *proof* 151 graphs (solved states). Live probing (NAXOS_eCK, NSLPK3 case-distinction graphs) 152 adds one more shape: 153 - **`invtrapezium`** — a `(#var, idx)` placeholder for an **unresolved graph node** 154 referenced by a still-open premise, e.g. `n9[label="(#i, 0)",shape="invtrapezium"]`. 155 `#var` is the (temporal) node variable, `idx` the premise index; the node is fed 156 by a structural edge from the conclusion that must satisfy that premise 157 (`n5:n2 -> n9[style="bold",weight="10.0",color="gray50"]`). Appears in both the 158 compressed default and the uncompressed variant, in every source-case graph 159 tested. Attr order = ellipse's (`label,shape`). 160 - **`trapezium`** (spec-named dual: an unresolved *source* node feeding a present 161 premise) was **NOT observed** in any probe (Session-3 NAXOS/NSLPK3 case graphs, 162 Session-5 re-scan of NSLPK3 `cases/{raw,refined}/i/j` for i≤12,j≤8) nor anywhere 163 in the corpus/manifests. The invtrapezium is the *conclusion→absent-node* case 164 (its edge points **into** it: `n5:n2 -> n6`), i.e. the "conclusion feeding a node 165 absent from the graph"; the un-seen dual would be the mirror (`absent -> premise`). 166 Recorded unobserved; `GraphNode::Shaped{label,shape,color}` (generic) can emit any 167 shape a caller supplies. 168 169 ### 3g. Bare-timepoint ellipse + the `#last` designated timepoint (§ Round-5 item 3) 170 An uncolored ellipse whose label is a bare timepoint variable `#<var>`: 171 `n<ID>[label="#<var>",shape="ellipse"];`. Corpus census of ellipse labels found, 172 besides the `!KU(..)@#t` / `#t:rule[..]` / `Action(..)@#t` forms, bare-timepoint 173 nodes `#i` (1328), `#decrypt` (790), `#t0…#t6`, `#j`, `#j.1`, and the **designated 174 last timepoint `#last`** (107, e.g. `24a119958f784d43.dot`). `#last` arises when a 175 constraint system carries a last timepoint (induction / trace-property proofs); it 176 is the target of a `color="black",style="dashed"` before-edge. All render as the 177 plain uncolored ellipse above. Modelled as `GraphNode::Temporal{var}` (with 178 `GraphNode::last()` for `#last`). 179 180 ### 3e. Node-id / port allocation (§ Round-3 item 3) — byte-verified over 12 022 181 One global monotonic counter, ids handed out in **emission order** so file order == 182 id order. For each node in order: 183 - a **record** takes one id per cell (in cell order: premises, then the info cell, 184 then conclusions) and **then** one id for the node itself — so a `k`-cell record 185 occupies `n<p>…n<p+k-1>` (ports) and `n<p+k>` (node); 186 - every other kind (ellipse / plain / invtrapezium) takes exactly one id. 187 188 Example: a 4-prem/1-info/3-concl record takes ports `n0…n6`, node `n7`; the next 189 ellipse is `n8`. Verified 12022/12022 in `mine_ids.py` and again in Rust 190 (`tests/alloc_corpus.rs`, via `NodeIdAllocator`). 191 192 ### 3f. Record-cell term rendering + line wrapping (§ Round-3 item 1) 193 **Group structure** (byte-verified over 160 409 records): the label is 194 `{grp}|{grp}|{grp}` where the groups are the *non-empty* ones among 195 `[premises, info, conclusions]`, in that order; the **info** group is always 196 present (100 %). An empty premise or conclusion group is dropped (a source rule 197 renders `{info}|{concl}`). 198 - **Info cell**: `#<temporal> : <RuleName>` and, if the rule has action facts, 199 `[<action>, …]` (e.g. `#i : I_Complete[Complete( … ), …]`, or bare `#vf.5 : Fresh`). 200 - **Premise/conclusion cell**: a single fact. 201 - **Fact spacing**: a fact pads — `Name( a, b )`, and `Name( )` with no args; 202 a **function application** does not — `f(a, b)`; a **tuple** is `<a, b>`. Verified 203 against the corpus. 204 - **Escaping**: inside a record label, `< > { } |` each get a leading backslash; 205 everything else (quotes, `~ $ ^ * ⊕`, spaces) is literal. 206 - **Line wrapping FORMAT** (byte-exact). When a group breaks, physical lines are 207 separated by `\l` (with a trailing `\l`), and each continuation line is indented 208 with a ` ` run **equal to the column of the broken group's first element** 209 (just after `( ` for a fact, after `<` for a tuple, after `[` for an action 210 list). Verified across 188 192 wrapped cells. 211 - **Line wrapping DECISION — RESOLVED (Session 4): a fixed-width paragraph fill, 212 width = 87 columns, measured per fact from column 0.** The Round-3 claim ("first 213 line 5..87, not a flat width, not derivable") is **refuted**: it *is* a flat 214 width. Established by driving crafted single-node theories 215 (`Out(<'a01', …, 'aN'>)`, `Out(<'aa…a', 'y'>)`) through the live server and 216 sweeping the term width one column at a time: 217 - **W = 87 absolute, functor-invariant.** A fact stays on one line iff its flat 218 rendering is ≤ 87 columns; at 88 it breaks. 100	-- The Doc calculus
101	
102	{-
103	Laws for $$
104	~~~~~~~~~~~
105	<a1>    (x $$ y) $$ z   = x $$ (y $$ z)
106	<a2>    empty $$ x      = x
107	<a3>    x $$ empty      = x
108	
109	        ...ditto $+$...
110	
111	Laws for <>
112	~~~~~~~~~~~
113	<b1>    (x <> y) <> z   = x <> (y <> z)
114	<b2>    empty <> x      = empty
115	<b3>    x <> empty      = x
116	
117	        ...ditto <+>...
118	
119	Laws for text
120	~~~~~~~~~~~~~
121	<t1>    text s <> text t        = text (s++t)
122	<t2>    text "" <> x            = x, if x non-empty
123	
124	** because of law n6, t2 only holds if x doesn't
125	** start with `nest'.
126	
127	
128	Laws for nest
129	~~~~~~~~~~~~~
130	<n1>    nest 0 x                = x
131	<n2>    nest k (nest k' x)      = nest (k+k') x
132	<n3>    nest k (x <> y)         = nest k x <> nest k y
133	<n4>    nest k (x $$ y)         = nest k x $$ nest k y
134	<n5>    nest k empty            = empty
135	<n6>    x <> nest k y           = x <> y, if x non-empty
136	
137	** Note the side condition on <n6>!  It is this that
138	** makes it OK for empty to be a left unit for <>.
139	
140	Miscellaneous
141	~~~~~~~~~~~~~
142	<m1>    (text s <> x) $$ y = text s <> ((text "" <> x) $$
143	                                         nest (-length s) y)
144	
145	<m2>    (x $$ y) <> z = x $$ (y <> z)
146	        if y non-empty
147

149	Laws for list versions
150	~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
151	<l1>    sep (ps++[empty]++qs)   = sep (ps ++ qs)
152	        ...ditto hsep, hcat, vcat, fill...
153	
154	<l2>    nest k (sep ps) = sep (map (nest k) ps)
155	        ...ditto hsep, hcat, vcat, fill...
156	
157	Laws for oneLiner
158	~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
159	<o1>    oneLiner (nest k p) = nest k (oneLiner p)
160	<o2>    oneLiner (x <> y)   = oneLiner x <> oneLiner y
161	
162	You might think that the following verion of <m1> would
163	be neater:
164	
165	<3 NO>  (text s <> x) $$ y = text s <> ((empty <> x)) $$
166	                                         nest (-length s) y)

169

182	-- The Doc data type
183	
184	-- | The abstract type of documents. A Doc represents a /set/ of layouts. A Doc
185	-- with no occurrences of Union or NoDoc represents just one layout.
186	data Doc a
187	  = Empty                                            -- ^ An empty span, see 'empty'.
188	  | NilAbove (Doc a)                                 -- ^ @text "" $$ x@.
189	  | TextBeside !(AnnotDetails a) (Doc a)             -- ^ @text s <> x@.
190	  | Nest {-# UNPACK #-} !Int (Doc a)                 -- ^ @nest k x@.
191	  | Union (Doc a) (Doc a)                            -- ^ @ul `union` ur@.
192	  | NoDoc                                            -- ^ The empty set of documents.
193	  | Beside (Doc a) Bool (Doc a)                      -- ^ True <=> space between.
194	  | Above (Doc a) Bool (Doc a)                       -- ^ True <=> never overlap.
195	#if __GLASGOW_HASKELL__ >= 701
196	  deriving (Generic)
197	#endif
198	
199	{-
200	Here are the invariants:
201	
202	1) The argument of NilAbove is never Empty. Therefore a NilAbove occupies at
203	least two lines.
204	
205	2) The argument of @TextBeside@ is never @Nest@.
206	
207	3) The layouts of the two arguments of @Union@ both flatten to the same string.
208	
209	4) The arguments of @Union@ are either @TextBeside@, or @NilAbove@.
210	
211	5) A @NoDoc@ may only appear on the first line of the left argument of an
212	   union. Therefore, the right argument of an union can never be equivalent to
213	   the empty set (@NoDoc@).
214	
215	6) An empty document is always represented by @Empty@. It can't be hidden
216	   inside a @Nest@, or a @Union@ of two @Empty@s.
217	
218	7) The first line of every layout in the left argument of @Union@ is longer
219	   than the first line of any layout in the right argument. (1) ensures that
220	   the left argument has a first line. In view of (3), this invariant means
221	   that the right argument must have at least two lines.
222	
223	Notice the difference between
224	   * NoDoc (no documents)
225	   * Empty (one empty document; no height and no width)
226	   * text "" (a document containing the empty string; one line high, but has no
227	              width)
228	-}

235	-- Allows carrying non-pretty-printed data around in a @Doc@ that is attached
236	-- at particular points in the structure. Once the @Doc@ is render to an output
237	-- type (such as 'String'), we can also retrieve where in the rendered document
238	-- our annotations start and end (see 'Span' and 'renderSpans').
239	data AnnotDetails a = AnnotStart
240	                    | NoAnnot !TextDetails {-# UNPACK #-} !Int
241	                    | AnnotEnd a
242	                      deriving (Show,Eq)

244	instance Functor AnnotDetails where
245	  fmap _ AnnotStart     = AnnotStart
246	  fmap _ (NoAnnot d i)  = NoAnnot d i
247	  fmap f (AnnotEnd a)   = AnnotEnd (f a)

250	annotSize :: AnnotDetails a -> Int

252	annotSize _             = 0

255	-- point in a @Doc@.
256	data TextDetails = Chr  {-# UNPACK #-} !Char -- ^ A single Char fragment
257	                 | Str  String -- ^ A whole String fragment
258	                 | PStr String -- ^ Used to represent a Fast String fragment

260	                               --   Str constructor.

262	                 deriving (Show, Eq, Generic)
263	#endif

265	-- Combining @Doc@ values
266	#if __GLASGOW_HASKELL__ >= 800
267	instance Semi.Semigroup (Doc a) where
268	#ifndef TESTING
269	    (<>) = (Text.PrettyPrint.Annotated.HughesPJ.<>)
270	#else
271	    (<>) = (PrettyTestVersion.<>)
272	#endif
273

275	    mempty  = empty
276	    mappend = (Semi.<>)
277	#else
278	instance Monoid (Doc a) where
279	    mempty  = empty
280	    mappend = (<>)
281	#endif
282	
283	instance IsString (Doc a) where
284	    fromString = text
285	
286	instance Show (Doc a) where
287	  showsPrec _ doc cont = fullRender (mode style) (lineLength style)
288	                                    (ribbonsPerLine style)
289	                                    txtPrinter cont doc
290	
291	instance Eq (Doc a) where
292	  (==) = (==) `on` render
293	
294	instance Functor Doc where
295	  fmap _ Empty               = Empty
296	  fmap f (NilAbove d)        = NilAbove (fmap f d)
297	  fmap f (TextBeside td d)   = TextBeside (fmap f td) (fmap f d)
298	  fmap f (Nest k d)          = Nest k (fmap f d)
299	  fmap f (Union ur ul)       = Union (fmap f ur) (fmap f ul)
300	  fmap _ NoDoc               = NoDoc
301	  fmap f (Beside ld s rd)    = Beside (fmap f ld) s (fmap f rd)
302	  fmap f (Above ud s ld)     = Above (fmap f ud) s (fmap f ld)
303	
304	instance NFData a => NFData (Doc a) where
305	  rnf Empty               = ()
306	  rnf (NilAbove d)        = rnf d
307	  rnf (TextBeside td d)   = rnf td `seq` rnf d
308	  rnf (Nest k d)          = rnf k  `seq` rnf d
309	  rnf (Union ur ul)       = rnf ur `seq` rnf ul
310	  rnf NoDoc               = ()
311	  rnf (Beside ld s rd)    = rnf ld `seq` rnf s `seq` rnf rd
312	  rnf (Above ud s ld)     = rnf ud `seq` rnf s `seq` rnf ld
313	
314	instance NFData a => NFData (AnnotDetails a) where
315	  rnf AnnotStart     = ()

318

324	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
325	-- Values and Predicates on GDocs and TextDetails
326	
327	-- | Attach an annotation to a document.
328	annotate :: a -> Doc a -> Doc a
329	annotate a d = TextBeside AnnotStart
330	             $ beside (reduceDoc d) False
331	             $ TextBeside (AnnotEnd a) Empty

333	
334	-- | A document of height and width 1, containing a literal character.
335	char :: Char -> Doc a
336	char c = textBeside_ (NoAnnot (Chr c) 1) Empty
337	
338	-- | A document of height 1 containing a literal string.
339	-- 'text' satisfies the following laws:
340	--
341	-- * @'text' s '<>' 'text' t = 'text' (s'++'t)@
342	--
343	-- * @'text' \"\" '<>' x = x@, if @x@ non-empty
344	--
345	-- The side condition on the last law is necessary because @'text' \"\"@
346	-- has height 1, while 'empty' has no height.
347	text :: String -> Doc a
348	text s = case length s of {sl -> textBeside_ (NoAnnot (Str s) sl) Empty}
349	
350	-- | Same as @text@. Used to be used for Bytestrings.
351	ptext :: String -> Doc a
352	ptext s = case length s of {sl -> textBeside_ (NoAnnot (PStr s) sl) Empty}
353	
354	-- | Some text with any width. (@text s = sizedText (length s) s@)
355	sizedText :: Int -> String -> Doc a
356	sizedText l s = textBeside_ (NoAnnot (Str s) l) Empty
357	
358	-- | Some text, but without any width. Use for non-printing text
359	-- such as a HTML or Latex tags

363	-- | The empty document, with no height and no width.
364	-- 'empty' is the identity for '<>', '<+>', '$$' and '$+$', and anywhere
365	-- in the argument list for 'sep', 'hcat', 'hsep', 'vcat', 'fcat' etc.
366	empty :: Doc a
367	empty = Empty

372	isEmpty _     = False

374	-- | Produce spacing for indenting the amount specified.
375	--

377	indent :: Int -> String
378	indent !n = replicate n ' '
379	
380	{-
381	Q: What is the reason for negative indentation (i.e. argument to indent
382	   is < 0) ?
383	
384	A:
385	This indicates an error in the library client's code.
386	If we compose a <> b, and the first line of b is more indented than some
387	other lines of b, the law <n6> (<> eats nests) may cause the pretty
388	printer to produce an invalid layout:
389	
390	doc       |0123345
391	------------------
392	d1        |a...|
393	d2        |...b|
394	          |c...|
395	
396	d1<>d2    |ab..|
397	         c|....|
398	
399	Consider a <> b, let `s' be the length of the last line of `a', `k' the
400	indentation of the first line of b, and `k0' the indentation of the
401	left-most line b_i of b.
402	
403	The produced layout will have negative indentation if `k - k0 > s', as
404	the first line of b will be put on the (s+1)th column, effectively
405	translating b horizontally by (k-s). Now if the i^th line of b has an
406	indentation k0 < (k-s), it is translated out-of-page, causing
407	`negative indentation'.
408	-}

476	maybeQuotes :: Bool -> Doc a -> Doc a
477	maybeQuotes False = id
478	maybeQuotes True = quotes
479	
480	-- | Apply 'doubleQuotes' to 'Doc' if boolean is true.
481	maybeDoubleQuotes :: Bool -> Doc a -> Doc a
482	maybeDoubleQuotes False = id
483	maybeDoubleQuotes True = doubleQuotes

485	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
486	-- Structural operations on GDocs
487	
488	-- | Perform some simplification of a built up @GDoc@.
489	reduceDoc :: Doc a -> RDoc a
490	reduceDoc (Beside p g q) = beside p g (reduceDoc q)
491	reduceDoc (Above  p g q) = above  p g (reduceDoc q)
492	reduceDoc p              = p
493	
494	-- | List version of '<>'.
495	hcat :: [Doc a] -> Doc a
496	hcat = snd . reduceHoriz . foldr (\p q -> Beside p False q) empty
497	
498	-- | List version of '<+>'.
499	hsep :: [Doc a] -> Doc a
500	hsep = snd . reduceHoriz . foldr (\p q -> Beside p True q)  empty
501	
502	-- | List version of '$$'.
503	vcat :: [Doc a] -> Doc a
504	vcat = snd . reduceVert . foldr (\p q -> Above p False q) empty
505	
506	-- | Nest (or indent) a document by a given number of positions
507	-- (which may also be negative).  'nest' satisfies the laws:
508	--
509	-- * @'nest' 0 x = x@
510	--
511	-- * @'nest' k ('nest' k' x) = 'nest' (k+k') x@
512	--
513	-- * @'nest' k (x '<>' y) = 'nest' k z '<>' 'nest' k y@
514	--
515	-- * @'nest' k (x '$$' y) = 'nest' k x '$$' 'nest' k y@
516	--
517	-- * @'nest' k 'empty' = 'empty'@
518	--
519	-- * @x '<>' 'nest' k y = x '<>' y@, if @x@ non-empty
520	--
521	-- The side condition on the last law is needed because
522	-- 'empty' is a left identity for '<>'.
523	nest :: Int -> Doc a -> Doc a
524	nest k p = mkNest k (reduceDoc p)
525	
526	-- | @hang d1 n d2 = sep [d1, nest n d2]@
527	hang :: Doc a -> Int -> Doc a -> Doc a
528	hang d1 n d2 = sep [d1, nest n d2]
529	
530	-- | @punctuate p [d1, ... dn] = [d1 \<> p, d2 \<> p, ... dn-1 \<> p, dn]@
531	punctuate :: Doc a -> [Doc a] -> [Doc a]
532	punctuate _ []     = []
533	punctuate p (x:xs) = go x xs
534	                   where go y []     = [y]
535	                         go y (z:zs) = (y <> p) : go z zs
536	
537	-- mkNest checks for Nest's invariant that it doesn't have an Empty inside it
538	mkNest :: Int -> Doc a -> Doc a
539	mkNest k _ | k `seq` False = undefined
540	mkNest k (Nest k1 p)       = mkNest (k + k1) p
541	mkNest _ NoDoc             = NoDoc
542	mkNest _ Empty             = Empty
543	mkNest 0 p                 = p
544	mkNest k p                 = nest_ k p
545	
546	-- mkUnion checks for an empty document
547	mkUnion :: Doc a -> Doc a -> Doc a
548	mkUnion Empty _ = Empty
549	mkUnion p q     = p `union_` q
550	
551	data IsEmpty = IsEmpty | NotEmpty
552	
553	reduceHoriz :: Doc a -> (IsEmpty, Doc a)
554	reduceHoriz (Beside p g q) = eliminateEmpty Beside (snd (reduceHoriz p)) g (reduceHoriz q)
555	reduceHoriz doc            = (NotEmpty, doc)
556	
557	reduceVert :: Doc a -> (IsEmpty, Doc a)
558	reduceVert (Above  p g q) = eliminateEmpty Above  (snd (reduceVert p)) g (reduceVert q)
559	reduceVert doc            = (NotEmpty, doc)
560	
561	{-# INLINE eliminateEmpty #-}
562	eliminateEmpty ::
563	  (Doc a -> Bool -> Doc a -> Doc a) ->
564	  Doc a -> Bool -> (IsEmpty, Doc a) -> (IsEmpty, Doc a)
565	eliminateEmpty _    Empty _ q          = q
566	eliminateEmpty cons p     g q          =
567	  (NotEmpty,
568	   -- We're not empty whether or not q is empty, so for laziness-sake,
569	   -- after checking that p isn't empty, we put the NotEmpty result
570	   -- outside independent of q. This allows reduceAB to immediately
571	   -- return the appropriate constructor (Above or Beside) without
572	   -- forcing the entire nested Doc. This allows the foldr in vcat,
573	   -- hsep, and hcat to be lazy on its second argument, avoiding a
574	   -- stack overflow.
575	   case q of
576	     (NotEmpty, q') -> cons p g q'
577	     (IsEmpty, _) -> p)
578	
579	nilAbove_ :: RDoc a -> RDoc a
580	nilAbove_ = NilAbove
581	
582	-- | Arg of a TextBeside is always an RDoc.
583	textBeside_ :: AnnotDetails a -> RDoc a -> RDoc a
584	textBeside_  = TextBeside
585	
586	nest_ :: Int -> RDoc a -> RDoc a
587	nest_ = Nest
588	
589	union_ :: RDoc a -> RDoc a -> RDoc a
590	union_ = Union

593	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
594	-- Vertical composition @$$@
595	
596	-- | Above, except that if the last line of the first argument stops
597	-- at least one position before the first line of the second begins,
598	-- these two lines are overlapped.  For example:
599	--
600	-- >    text "hi" $$ nest 5 (text "there")
601	--
602	-- lays out as
603	--
604	-- >    hi   there
605	--
606	-- rather than
607	--
608	-- >    hi
609	-- >         there
610	--
611	-- '$$' is associative, with identity 'empty', and also satisfies
612	--
613	-- * @(x '$$' y) '<>' z = x '$$' (y '<>' z)@, if @y@ non-empty.
614	--
615	($$) :: Doc a -> Doc a -> Doc a
616	p $$  q = above_ p False q
617	
618	-- | Above, with no overlapping.
619	-- '$+$' is associative, with identity 'empty'.
620	($+$) :: Doc a -> Doc a -> Doc a
621	p $+$ q = above_ p True q
622	
623	above_ :: Doc a -> Bool -> Doc a -> Doc a
624	above_ p _ Empty = p
625	above_ Empty _ q = q
626	above_ p g q     = Above p g q
627	
628	above :: Doc a -> Bool -> RDoc a -> RDoc a
629	above (Above p g1 q1)  g2 q2 = above p g1 (above q1 g2 q2)
630	above p@(Beside{})     g  q  = aboveNest (reduceDoc p) g 0 (reduceDoc q)
631	above p g q                  = aboveNest p             g 0 (reduceDoc q)
632	
633	-- Specfication: aboveNest p g k q = p $g$ (nest k q)
634	aboveNest :: RDoc a -> Bool -> Int -> RDoc a -> RDoc a
635	aboveNest _                   _ k _ | k `seq` False = undefined
636	aboveNest NoDoc               _ _ _ = NoDoc
637	aboveNest (p1 `Union` p2)     g k q = aboveNest p1 g k q `union_`
638	                                      aboveNest p2 g k q
639	
640	aboveNest Empty               _ k q = mkNest k q
641	aboveNest (Nest k1 p)         g k q = nest_ k1 (aboveNest p g (k - k1) q)
642	                                  -- p can't be Empty, so no need for mkNest
643	
644	aboveNest (NilAbove p)        g k q = nilAbove_ (aboveNest p g k q)
645	aboveNest (TextBeside s p)    g k q = TextBeside s rest
646	                                    where
647	                                      !k1  = k - annotSize s
648	                                      rest = case p of
649	                                                Empty -> nilAboveNest g k1 q
650	                                                _     -> aboveNest  p g k1 q
651	
652	aboveNest (Above {})          _ _ _ = error "aboveNest Above"
653	aboveNest (Beside {})         _ _ _ = error "aboveNest Beside"
654	
655	-- Specification: text s <> nilaboveNest g k q
656	--              = text s <> (text "" $g$ nest k q)
657	nilAboveNest :: Bool -> Int -> RDoc a -> RDoc a
658	nilAboveNest _ k _           | k `seq` False = undefined
659	nilAboveNest _ _ Empty       = Empty
660	                               -- Here's why the "text s <>" is in the spec!
661	nilAboveNest g k (Nest k1 q) = nilAboveNest g (k + k1) q
662	nilAboveNest g k q           | not g && k > 0      -- No newline if no overlap
663	                             = textBeside_ (NoAnnot (Str (indent k)) k) q
664	                             | otherwise           -- Put them really above
665	                             = nilAbove_ (mkNest k q)
666	
667	
668	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
669	-- Horizontal composition @<>@
670	
671	-- We intentionally avoid Data.Monoid.(<>) here due to interactions of
672	-- Data.Monoid.(<>) and (<+>).  See
673	-- http://www.haskell.org/pipermail/libraries/2011-November/017066.html
674	
675	-- | Beside.
676	-- '<>' is associative, with identity 'empty'.
677	(<>) :: Doc a -> Doc a -> Doc a
678	p <>  q = beside_ p False q
679	
680	-- | Beside, separated by space, unless one of the arguments is 'empty'.
681	-- '<+>' is associative, with identity 'empty'.
682	(<+>) :: Doc a -> Doc a -> Doc a
683	p <+> q = beside_ p True  q
684	
685	beside_ :: Doc a -> Bool -> Doc a -> Doc a
686	beside_ p _ Empty = p
687	beside_ Empty _ q = q
688	beside_ p g q     = Beside p g q
689	
690	-- Specification: beside g p q = p <g> q
691	beside :: Doc a -> Bool -> RDoc a -> RDoc a
692	beside NoDoc               _ _   = NoDoc
693	beside (p1 `Union` p2)     g q   = beside p1 g q `union_` beside p2 g q
694	beside Empty               _ q   = q
695	beside (Nest k p)          g q   = nest_ k $! beside p g q
696	beside p@(Beside p1 g1 q1) g2 q2
697	         | g1 == g2              = beside p1 g1 $! beside q1 g2 q2
698	         | otherwise             = beside (reduceDoc p) g2 q2
699	beside p@(Above{})         g q   = let !d = reduceDoc p in beside d g q
700	beside (NilAbove p)        g q   = nilAbove_ $! beside p g q
701	beside (TextBeside t p)    g q   = TextBeside t rest
702	                               where
703	                                  rest = case p of
704	                                           Empty -> nilBeside g q
705	                                           _     -> beside p g q
706	
707	-- Specification: text "" <> nilBeside g p
708	--              = text "" <g> p
709	nilBeside :: Bool -> RDoc a -> RDoc a
710	nilBeside _ Empty         = Empty -- Hence the text "" in the spec
711	nilBeside g (Nest _ p)    = nilBeside g p
712	nilBeside g p | g         = textBeside_ spaceText p
713	              | otherwise = p

716	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
717	-- Separate, @sep@
718	
719	-- Specification: sep ps  = oneLiner (hsep ps)
720	--                         `union`
721	--                          vcat ps
722	
723	-- | Either 'hsep' or 'vcat'.
724	sep  :: [Doc a] -> Doc a
725	sep = sepX True   -- Separate with spaces
726	
727	-- | Either 'hcat' or 'vcat'.
728	cat :: [Doc a] -> Doc a
729	cat = sepX False  -- Don't
730	
731	sepX :: Bool -> [Doc a] -> Doc a
732	sepX _ []     = empty
733	sepX x (p:ps) = sep1 x (reduceDoc p) 0 ps
734	
735	
736	-- Specification: sep1 g k ys = sep (x : map (nest k) ys)
737	--                            = oneLiner (x <g> nest k (hsep ys))
738	--                              `union` x $$ nest k (vcat ys)
739	sep1 :: Bool -> RDoc a -> Int -> [Doc a] -> RDoc a
740	sep1 _ _                   k _  | k `seq` False = undefined
741	sep1 _ NoDoc               _ _  = NoDoc
742	sep1 g (p `Union` q)       k ys = sep1 g p k ys `union_`
743	                                  aboveNest q False k (reduceDoc (vcat ys))
744	
745	sep1 g Empty               k ys = mkNest k (sepX g ys)
746	sep1 g (Nest n p)          k ys = nest_ n (sep1 g p (k - n) ys)
747	
748	sep1 _ (NilAbove p)        k ys = nilAbove_
749	                                  (aboveNest p False k (reduceDoc (vcat ys)))
750	sep1 g (TextBeside s p) k ys    = textBeside_ s (sepNB g p (k - annotSize s) ys)
751	sep1 _ (Above {})          _ _  = error "sep1 Above"
752	sep1 _ (Beside {})         _ _  = error "sep1 Beside"
753	
754	-- Specification: sepNB p k ys = sep1 (text "" <> p) k ys
755	-- Called when we have already found some text in the first item
756	-- We have to eat up nests
757	sepNB :: Bool -> Doc a -> Int -> [Doc a] -> Doc a
758	sepNB g (Nest _ p) k ys
759	  = sepNB g p k ys -- Never triggered, because of invariant (2)
760	sepNB g Empty k ys
761	  = oneLiner (nilBeside g (reduceDoc rest)) `mkUnion`
762	    -- XXX: TODO: PRETTY: Used to use True here (but GHC used False...)
763	    nilAboveNest False k (reduceDoc (vcat ys))
764	  where
765	    rest | g         = hsep ys
766	         | otherwise = hcat ys
767	sepNB g p k ys
768	  = sep1 g p k ys

771	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
772	-- @fill@
773	
774	-- | \"Paragraph fill\" version of 'cat'.
775	fcat :: [Doc a] -> Doc a
776	fcat = fill False
777	
778	-- | \"Paragraph fill\" version of 'sep'.
779	fsep :: [Doc a] -> Doc a
780	fsep = fill True
781	
782	-- Specification:
783	--
784	-- fill g docs = fillIndent 0 docs
785	--
786	-- fillIndent k [] = []
787	-- fillIndent k [p] = p
788	-- fillIndent k (p1:p2:ps) =
789	--    oneLiner p1 <g> fillIndent (k + length p1 + g ? 1 : 0)
790	--                               (remove_nests (oneLiner p2) : ps)
791	--     `Union`
792	--    (p1 $*$ nest (-k) (fillIndent 0 ps))
793	--
794	-- $*$ is defined for layouts (not Docs) as
795	-- layout1 $*$ layout2 | hasMoreThanOneLine layout1 = layout1 $$ layout2
796	--                     | otherwise                  = layout1 $+$ layout2
797	
798	fill :: Bool -> [Doc a] -> RDoc a
799	fill _ []     = empty
800	fill g (p:ps) = fill1 g (reduceDoc p) 0 ps
801	
802	fill1 :: Bool -> RDoc a -> Int -> [Doc a] -> Doc a
803	fill1 _ _                   k _  | k `seq` False = undefined
804	fill1 _ NoDoc               _ _  = NoDoc
805	fill1 g (p `Union` q)       k ys = fill1 g p k ys `union_`
806	                                   aboveNest q False k (fill g ys)
807	fill1 g Empty               k ys = mkNest k (fill g ys)
808	fill1 g (Nest n p)          k ys = nest_ n (fill1 g p (k - n) ys)
809	fill1 g (NilAbove p)        k ys = nilAbove_ (aboveNest p False k (fill g ys))
810	fill1 g (TextBeside s p)    k ys = textBeside_ s (fillNB g p (k - annotSize s) ys)
811	fill1 _ (Above {})          _ _  = error "fill1 Above"
812	fill1 _ (Beside {})         _ _  = error "fill1 Beside"
813	
814	fillNB :: Bool -> Doc a -> Int -> [Doc a] -> Doc a
815	fillNB _ _           k _  | k `seq` False = undefined
816	fillNB g (Nest _ p)  k ys   = fillNB g p k ys
817	                              -- Never triggered, because of invariant (2)
818	fillNB _ Empty _ []         = Empty
819	fillNB g Empty k (Empty:ys) = fillNB g Empty k ys
820	fillNB g Empty k (y:ys)     = fillNBE g k y ys
821	fillNB g p k ys             = fill1 g p k ys
822	
823	
824	fillNBE :: Bool -> Int -> Doc a -> [Doc a] -> Doc a
825	fillNBE g k y ys
826	  = nilBeside g (fill1 g ((elideNest . oneLiner . reduceDoc) y) k' ys)
827	    -- XXX: TODO: PRETTY: Used to use True here (but GHC used False...)
828	    `mkUnion` nilAboveNest False k (fill g (y:ys))
829	  where k' = if g then k - 1 else k
830	
831	elideNest :: Doc a -> Doc a
832	elideNest (Nest _ d) = d
833	elideNest d          = d
834

836	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
837	-- Selecting the best layout
838	
839	best :: Int   -- Line length.
840	     -> Int   -- Ribbon length.
841	     -> RDoc a
842	     -> RDoc a  -- No unions in here!.
843	best w0 r = get w0
844	  where
845	    get w _ | w == 0 && False = undefined
846	    get _ Empty               = Empty
847	    get _ NoDoc               = NoDoc
848	    get w (NilAbove p)        = nilAbove_ (get w p)
849	    get w (TextBeside s p)    = textBeside_ s (get1 w (annotSize s) p)
850	    get w (Nest k p)          = nest_ k (get (w - k) p)
851	    get w (p `Union` q)       = nicest w r (get w p) (get w q)
852	    get _ (Above {})          = error "best get Above"
853	    get _ (Beside {})         = error "best get Beside"
854	
855	    get1 w _ _ | w == 0 && False  = undefined
856	    get1 _ _  Empty               = Empty
857	    get1 _ _  NoDoc               = NoDoc
858	    get1 w sl (NilAbove p)        = nilAbove_ (get (w - sl) p)
859	    get1 w sl (TextBeside s p)    = textBeside_ s (get1 w (sl + annotSize s) p)
860	    get1 w sl (Nest _ p)          = get1 w sl p
861	    get1 w sl (p `Union` q)       = nicest1 w r sl (get1 w sl p)
862	                                                   (get1 w sl q)
863	    get1 _ _  (Above {})          = error "best get1 Above"
864	    get1 _ _  (Beside {})         = error "best get1 Beside"
865	
866	nicest :: Int -> Int -> Doc a -> Doc a -> Doc a
867	nicest !w !r = nicest1 w r 0
868	
869	nicest1 :: Int -> Int -> Int -> Doc a -> Doc a -> Doc a
870	nicest1 !w !r !sl p q | fits ((w `min` r) - sl) p = p
871	                      | otherwise                 = q
872	
873	fits :: Int  -- Space available
874	     -> Doc a
875	     -> Bool -- True if *first line* of Doc fits in space available
876	fits n _ | n < 0           = False
877	fits _ NoDoc               = False
878	fits _ Empty               = True
879	fits _ (NilAbove _)        = True
880	fits n (TextBeside s p)    = fits (n - annotSize s) p
881	fits _ (Above {})          = error "fits Above"
882	fits _ (Beside {})         = error "fits Beside"
883	fits _ (Union {})          = error "fits Union"
884	fits _ (Nest {})           = error "fits Nest"
885	
886	-- | @first@ returns its first argument if it is non-empty, otherwise its
887	-- second.
888	first :: Doc a -> Doc a -> Doc a
889	first p q | nonEmptySet p = p -- unused, because (get OneLineMode) is unused
890	          | otherwise     = q
891	
892	nonEmptySet :: Doc a -> Bool
893	nonEmptySet NoDoc              = False
894	nonEmptySet (_ `Union` _)      = True
895	nonEmptySet Empty              = True
896	nonEmptySet (NilAbove _)       = True
897	nonEmptySet (TextBeside _ p)   = nonEmptySet p
898	nonEmptySet (Nest _ p)         = nonEmptySet p
899	nonEmptySet (Above {})         = error "nonEmptySet Above"
900	nonEmptySet (Beside {})        = error "nonEmptySet Beside"
901	
902	-- @oneLiner@ returns the one-line members of the given set of @GDoc@s.
903	oneLiner :: Doc a -> Doc a
904	oneLiner NoDoc               = NoDoc
905	oneLiner Empty               = Empty
906	oneLiner (NilAbove _)        = NoDoc
907	oneLiner (TextBeside s p)    = textBeside_ s (oneLiner p)
908	oneLiner (Nest k p)          = nest_ k (oneLiner p)
909	oneLiner (p `Union` _)       = oneLiner p
910	oneLiner (Above {})          = error "oneLiner Above"
911	oneLiner (Beside {})         = error "oneLiner Beside"

914	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
915	-- Rendering
916	
917	-- | A rendering style. Allows us to specify constraints to choose among the
918	-- many different rendering options.
919	data Style
920	  = Style { mode           :: Mode
921	            -- ^ The rendering mode.
922	          , lineLength     :: Int
923	            -- ^ Maximum length of a line, in characters.
924	          , ribbonsPerLine :: Float
925	            -- ^ Ratio of line length to ribbon length. A ribbon refers to the
926	            -- characters on a line /excluding/ indentation. So a 'lineLength'
927	            -- of 100, with a 'ribbonsPerLine' of @2.0@ would only allow up to
928	            -- 50 characters of ribbon to be displayed on a line, while
929	            -- allowing it to be indented up to 50 characters.
930	          }
931	#if __GLASGOW_HASKELL__ >= 701
932	  deriving (Show, Eq, Generic)
933	#endif
934	
935	-- | The default style (@mode=PageMode, lineLength=100, ribbonsPerLine=1.5@).
936	style :: Style
937	style = Style { lineLength = 100, ribbonsPerLine = 1.5, mode = PageMode }
938	
939	-- | Rendering mode.
940	data Mode = PageMode    
941	            -- ^ Normal rendering ('lineLength' and 'ribbonsPerLine'
942	            -- respected').
943	          | ZigZagMode  
944	            -- ^ With zig-zag cuts.
945	          | LeftMode    
946	            -- ^ No indentation, infinitely long lines ('lineLength' ignored),
947	            -- but explicit new lines, i.e., @text "one" $$ text "two"@, are
948	            -- respected.
949	          | OneLineMode 
950	            -- ^ All on one line, 'lineLength' ignored and explicit new lines
951	            -- (@$$@) are turned into spaces.
952	#if __GLASGOW_HASKELL__ >= 701
953	          deriving (Show, Eq, Generic)
954	#endif
955	
956	-- | Render the @Doc@ to a String using the default @Style@ (see 'style').
957	render :: Doc a -> String
958	render = fullRender (mode style) (lineLength style) (ribbonsPerLine style)
959	                    txtPrinter ""
960	
961	-- | Render the @Doc@ to a String using the given @Style@.
962	renderStyle :: Style -> Doc a -> String
963	renderStyle s = fullRender (mode s) (lineLength s) (ribbonsPerLine s)
964	                txtPrinter ""
965	
966	-- | Default TextDetails printer.
967	txtPrinter :: TextDetails -> String -> String
968	txtPrinter (Chr c)   s  = c:s
969	txtPrinter (Str s1)  s2 = s1 ++ s2
970	txtPrinter (PStr s1) s2 = s1 ++ s2
971	
972	-- | The general rendering interface. Please refer to the @Style@ and @Mode@
973	-- types for a description of rendering mode, line length and ribbons.
974	fullRender :: Mode                    -- ^ Rendering mode.
975	           -> Int                     -- ^ Line length.
976	           -> Float                   -- ^ Ribbons per line.
977	           -> (TextDetails -> a -> a) -- ^ What to do with text.
978	           -> a                       -- ^ What to do at the end.
979	           -> Doc b                   -- ^ The document.
980	           -> a                       -- ^ Result.
981	fullRender m l r txt = fullRenderAnn m l r annTxt
982	  where
983	  annTxt (NoAnnot s _) = txt s
984	  annTxt _             = id
985	
986	-- | The general rendering interface, supporting annotations. Please refer to
987	-- the @Style@ and @Mode@ types for a description of rendering mode, line
988	-- length and ribbons.
989	fullRenderAnn :: Mode                       -- ^ Rendering mode.
990	              -> Int                        -- ^ Line length.
991	              -> Float                      -- ^ Ribbons per line.
992	              -> (AnnotDetails b -> a -> a) -- ^ What to do with text.
993	              -> a                          -- ^ What to do at the end.
994	              -> Doc b                      -- ^ The document.
995	              -> a                          -- ^ Result.
996	fullRenderAnn OneLineMode _ _ txt end doc
997	  = easyDisplay spaceText (\_ y -> y) txt end (reduceDoc doc)
998	fullRenderAnn LeftMode    _ _ txt end doc
999	  = easyDisplay nlText first txt end (reduceDoc doc)
1000	
1001	fullRenderAnn m lineLen ribbons txt rest doc
1002	  = display m lineLen ribbonLen txt rest doc'
1003	  where
1004	    doc' = best bestLineLen ribbonLen (reduceDoc doc)
1005	
1006	    bestLineLen, ribbonLen :: Int
1007	    ribbonLen   = round (fromIntegral lineLen / ribbons)
1008	    bestLineLen = case m of
1009	                      ZigZagMode -> maxBound
1010	                      _          -> lineLen
1011	
1012	easyDisplay :: AnnotDetails b
1013	             -> (Doc b -> Doc b -> Doc b)
1014	             -> (AnnotDetails b -> a -> a)
1015	             -> a
1016	             -> Doc b
1017	             -> a
1018	easyDisplay nlSpaceText choose txt end
1019	  = lay
1020	  where
1021	    lay NoDoc              = error "easyDisplay: NoDoc"
1022	    lay (Union p q)        = lay (choose p q)
1023	    lay (Nest _ p)         = lay p
1024	    lay Empty              = end
1025	    lay (NilAbove p)       = nlSpaceText `txt` lay p
1026	    lay (TextBeside s p)   = s `txt` lay p
1027	    lay (Above {})         = error "easyDisplay Above"
1028	    lay (Beside {})        = error "easyDisplay Beside"
1029	
1030	display :: Mode -> Int -> Int -> (AnnotDetails b -> a -> a) -> a -> Doc b -> a
1031	display m !page_width !ribbon_width txt end doc
1032	  = case page_width - ribbon_width of { gap_width ->
1033	    case gap_width `quot` 2 of { shift ->
1034	    let
1035	        lay k _            | k `seq` False = undefined
1036	        lay k (Nest k1 p)  = lay (k + k1) p
1037	        lay _ Empty        = end
1038	        lay k (NilAbove p) = nlText `txt` lay k p
1039	        lay k (TextBeside s p)
1040	            = case m of
1041	                    ZigZagMode |  k >= gap_width
1042	                               -> nlText `txt` (
1043	                                  NoAnnot (Str (replicate shift '/')) shift `txt` (
1044	                                  nlText `txt`
1045	                                  lay1 (k - shift) s p ))
1046	
1047	                               |  k < 0
1048	                               -> nlText `txt` (
1049	                                  NoAnnot (Str (replicate shift '\\')) shift `txt` (
1050	                                  nlText `txt`
1051	                                  lay1 (k + shift) s p ))
1052	
1053	                    _ -> lay1 k s p
1054	
1055	        lay _ (Above {})   = error "display lay Above"
1056	        lay _ (Beside {})  = error "display lay Beside"
1057	        lay _ NoDoc        = error "display lay NoDoc"
1058	        lay _ (Union {})   = error "display lay Union"
1059	
1060	        lay1 !k s p        = let !r = k + annotSize s
1061	                             in NoAnnot (Str (indent k)) k `txt` (s `txt` lay2 r p)
1062	
1063	        lay2 k _ | k `seq` False   = undefined
1064	        lay2 k (NilAbove p)        = nlText `txt` lay k p
1065	        lay2 k (TextBeside s p)    = s `txt` lay2 (k + annotSize s) p
1066	        lay2 k (Nest _ p)          = lay2 k p
1067	        lay2 _ Empty               = end
1068	        lay2 _ (Above {})          = error "display lay2 Above"
1069	        lay2 _ (Beside {})         = error "display lay2 Beside"
1070	        lay2 _ NoDoc               = error "display lay2 NoDoc"
1071	        lay2 _ (Union {})          = error "display lay2 Union"
1072	    in
1073	    lay 0 doc
1074	    }}
1075	
1076	
1077	
1078	-- Rendering Annotations -------------------------------------------------------
1079	
1080	-- | A @Span@ represents the result of an annotation after a @Doc@ has been
1081	-- rendered, capturing where the annotation now starts and ends in the rendered
1082	-- output.
1083	data Span a = Span { spanStart      :: !Int
1084	                   , spanLength     :: !Int
1085	                   , spanAnnotation :: a
1086	                   } deriving (Show,Eq)
1087	
1088	instance Functor Span where
1089	  fmap f (Span x y a) = Span x y (f a)
1090	
1091	
1092	-- State required for generating document spans.
1093	data Spans a = Spans { sOffset :: !Int
1094	                       -- ^ Current offset from the end of the document.
1095	                     , sStack  :: [Int -> Span a]
1096	                       -- ^ Currently open spans.
1097	                     , sSpans  :: [Span a]
1098	                       -- ^ Collected annotation regions.
1099	                     , sOutput :: String
1100	                       -- ^ Collected output.
1101	                     }
1102	
1103	-- | Render an annotated @Doc@ to a String and list of annotations (see 'Span')
1104	-- using the default @Style@ (see 'style').
1105	renderSpans :: Doc ann -> (String,[Span ann])
1106	renderSpans  = finalize
1107	             . fullRenderAnn (mode style) (lineLength style) (ribbonsPerLine style)
1108	                  spanPrinter
1109	                  Spans { sOffset = 0, sStack = [], sSpans = [], sOutput = "" }
1110	  where
1111	
1112	  finalize (Spans size _ spans out) = (out, map adjust spans)
1113	    where
1114	    adjust s = s { spanStart = size - spanStart s }
1115	
1116	  mkSpan a end start = Span { spanStart      = start
1117	                            , spanLength     = start - end
1118	                              -- this seems wrong, but remember that it's
1119	                              -- working backwards at this point
1120	                            , spanAnnotation = a }
1121	
1122	  -- the document gets generated in reverse, which is why the starting
1123	  -- annotation ends the annotation.
1124	  spanPrinter AnnotStart s =
1125	    case sStack s of
1126	      sp : rest -> s { sSpans = sp (sOffset s) : sSpans s, sStack = rest }
1127	      _         -> error "renderSpans: stack underflow"
1128	
1129	  spanPrinter (AnnotEnd a) s =
1130	    s { sStack = mkSpan a (sOffset s) : sStack s }
1131	
1132	  spanPrinter (NoAnnot td l) s =
1133	    case td of
1134	      Chr  c -> s { sOutput = c  : sOutput s, sOffset = sOffset s + l }
1135	      Str  t -> s { sOutput = t ++ sOutput s, sOffset = sOffset s + l }
1136	      PStr t -> s { sOutput = t ++ sOutput s, sOffset = sOffset s + l }
1137	
1138	
1139	-- | Render out a String, interpreting the annotations as part of the resulting
1140	-- document.
1141	--
1142	-- /IMPORTANT/: the size of the annotation string does NOT figure into the
1143	-- layout of the document, so the document will lay out as though the
1144	-- annotations are not present.
1145	renderDecorated :: (ann -> String) -- ^ Starting an annotation.
1146	                -> (ann -> String) -- ^ Ending an annotation.
1147	                -> Doc ann -> String
1148	renderDecorated startAnn endAnn =
1149	  finalize . fullRenderAnn (mode style) (lineLength style) (ribbonsPerLine style)
1150	                 annPrinter
1151	                 ("", [])
1152	  where
1153	  annPrinter AnnotStart (rest,stack) =
1154	    case stack of
1155	      a : as -> (startAnn a ++ rest, as)
1156	      _      -> error "renderDecorated: stack underflow"
1157	
1158	  annPrinter (AnnotEnd a) (rest,stack) =
1159	    (endAnn a ++ rest, a : stack)
1160	
1161	  annPrinter (NoAnnot s _) (rest,stack) =
1162	    (txtPrinter s rest, stack)
1163	
1164	  finalize (str,_) = str
1165	
1166	
1167	-- | Render a document with annotations, by interpreting the start and end of
1168	-- the annotations, as well as the text details in the context of a monad.
1169	renderDecoratedM :: Monad m
1170	                 => (ann    -> m r) -- ^ Starting an annotation.
1171	                 -> (ann    -> m r) -- ^ Ending an annotation.
1172	                 -> (String -> m r) -- ^ Text formatting.
1173	                 -> m r             -- ^ Document end.
1174	                 -> Doc ann -> m r
1175	renderDecoratedM startAnn endAnn txt docEnd =
1176	  finalize . fullRenderAnn (mode style) (lineLength style) (ribbonsPerLine style)
1177	                 annPrinter
1178	                 (docEnd, [])
1179	  where
1180	  annPrinter AnnotStart (rest,stack) =
1181	    case stack of
1182	      a : as -> (startAnn a >> rest, as)
1183	      _      -> error "renderDecorated: stack underflow"
1184	
1185	  annPrinter (AnnotEnd a) (rest,stack) =
1186	    (endAnn a >> rest, a : stack)
1187	
1188	  annPrinter (NoAnnot td _) (rest,stack) =
1189	    case td of
1190	      Chr  c -> (txt [c] >> rest, stack)
1191	      Str  s -> (txt s   >> rest, stack)
1192	      PStr s -> (txt s   >> rest, stack)
1193	
1194	  finalize (m,_) = m

#!/usr/bin/env python3
# Autoprove each lemma of a served theory and fetch its proof-state graph DOT.
# Usage: drive.py <port> <lemma1> [lemma2 ...]   -> prints "LEMMA\t<dotpath>" lines
import sys, re, json, urllib.request, os
PORT=sys.argv[1]; LEMMAS=sys.argv[2:]
BASE=f"http://127.0.0.1:{PORT}"
OUT=os.environ.get("OUTDIR","/tmp/probe_dots"); os.makedirs(OUT,exist_ok=True)
def get(path, timeout=300):
    with urllib.request.urlopen(BASE+path, timeout=timeout) as r:
        return r.read().decode("utf-8","replace")
def latest_index():
    idx=get("/")
    ms=re.findall(r'/thy/trace/(\d+)/', idx)
    return max(int(x) for x in ms) if ms else 1
for L in LEMMAS:
    i=latest_index()
    # autoprove this lemma from the current latest theory
    try:
        r=get(f"/thy/trace/{i}/autoprove/idfs/0/False/proof/{L}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"{L}\tAUTOPROVE_ERR {e}"); continue
    # response is JSON with a redirect to the new theory index
    ni=i
    m=re.search(r'/thy/trace/(\d+)/', r)
    if m: ni=int(m.group(1))
    try:
        dot=get(f"/thy/trace/{ni}/interactive-graph-def/proof/{L}/_")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"{L}\tGRAPH_ERR {e}"); continue
    p=os.path.join(OUT,f"{L}.dot")
    open(p,"w").write(dot)
    print(f"{L}\t{p}\t{len(dot)}")

41	    t=cell.replace(' ','')

48	    lost=len(re.findall(r',\\l',cell))+len(re.findall(r'\\l\)',cell))
49	    return len(unescape(cell.replace('\\l','').replace(' ',''))) + lost

52	    lines=layout_cell(flat,budget)

54	    return "|".join(f"{ind}:{txt}" for ind,txt in lines)

67	    others=sum(f for j,f in enumerate(flats) if j!=i)

81	    # each sibling occupies min(flat, its flat-sum budget): a wrapping sibling
82	    # fills its allocated budget, a fitting one its flat.
83	    n=len(flats)
84	    contrib=[min(flats[j], cell_budget(flats,j)) for j in range(n)]
85	    return [max(87-(sum(contrib)-contrib[i]),20) for i in range(n)]
86	
87	def greedy_budgets(flats):
88	    # smallest-flat-first; a processed sibling occupies min(flat, its assigned
89	    # budget), an un-processed (wider) one its full flat.
90	    n=len(flats)
91	    order=sorted(range(n), key=lambda i: flats[i])
92	    contrib=list(flats); budget=[0]*n
93	    for i in order:
94	        others=sum(contrib[j] for j in range(n) if j!=i)
95	        b=max(87-others,20); budget[i]=b
96	        contrib[i]=min(flats[i], b)

114	            flat_cells=[dewrap(c) for c in cells]
115	            occb=occ_budgets(flat_cells, flats)
116	            grb=greedy_budgets(flats)

119	                tot+=1

122	                def eff(b): return min(b if flats[k]>bf else bf, flats[k]-1)
123	                rf=render_lines(flat_cells[k], min(bf, flats[k]-1))
124	                ro=render_lines(flat_cells[k], eff(occb[k]))
125	                rg=render_lines(flat_cells[k], eff(grb[k]))
126	                mf=(rf==act); mo=(ro==act); mm=(rg==act)
127	                match_flat+=mf; match_occ+=mo; match_min+=mm

129	print(f"flatsum            budget match: {match_flat} ({100*match_flat/tot:.3f}%)")
130	print(f"occ-greedy         budget match: {match_occ} ({100*match_occ/tot:.3f}%)")

#!/usr/bin/env python3
# Generate a probe theory: 2-cell conclusion groups [Big(K distinct 2-char vars),
# Sib(single public constant of controlled length)] to measure how a group's
# cells share the row. Distinct vars / occ-1 constants => no abbreviation.
import sys, itertools

# distinct 2-char var names: aa, ab, ... (avoid clashing with builtins)
def varname(i):
    a = "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz"
    return a[i//26] + a[i%26]

def big(K):
    elems = ", ".join(varname(i) for i in range(K))
    return f"Big(<{elems}>)"

# single public constant of P a-chars => Sib flat = len("Sib( ") + (P+2) + len(" )")
def sib(P, idx):
    return f"Sib('{'a'*P}{idx}')"   # trailing idx keeps constants distinct (occ 1)

rules = []
lemmas = []
# sweep: K in a few sizes, sib length P in a range
combos = []
n = 0
for K in [8, 10, 12, 16, 20]:
    for P in [1, 3, 5, 8, 10, 12, 15, 18, 20, 25, 30, 40, 50, 60]:
        name = f"R_{K}_{P}"
        # unique fresh so the rule fires; premise In feeds the vars
        invars = ", ".join(varname(i) for i in range(K))
        rules.append(
            f"rule {name}:\n"
            f"  [ Fr(~n{n}), In(<{invars}>) ]\n"
            f"  --[ Fire{n}() ]->\n"
            f"  [ {big(K)}, {sib(P, n)} ]\n"
        )
        lemmas.append(f"lemma l_{name}:\n  exists-trace \"Ex #i. Fire{n}() @ #i\"\n")
        combos.append((name, K, P))
        n += 1

with open("probe.spthy", "w") as f:
    f.write("theory GroupShareProbe\nbegin\n\nbuiltins: hashing\n\n")
    for r in rules: f.write(r + "\n")
    for l in lemmas: f.write(l + "\n")
    f.write("end\n")

with open("probe.combos", "w") as f:
    for name, K, P in combos:
        f.write(f"{name}\t{K}\t{P}\n")

print(f"wrote probe.spthy with {len(rules)} rules")

#!/usr/bin/env python3
# For each probe record, extract conclusion cells [Big, Sib]; for each cell,
# back out the effective line width W that the exact engine needs to reproduce
# the captured bytes. Report W vs (flat_Big, flat_Sib, group_total, sib_wraps).
import re, glob, subprocess, os
LAYOUT="/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/workspace/graph-clean/target/release/layout_at"
DOTS="/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/workspace/r8/probe_dots"

def split_top(s, sep='|'):
    parts=[]; depth=0; cur=[]; i=0
    while i<len(s):
        c=s[i]
        if c=='\\' and i+1<len(s): cur.append(s[i:i+2]); i+=2; continue
        if c=='{': depth+=1; cur.append(c)
        elif c=='}': depth-=1; cur.append(c)
        elif c==sep and depth==0: parts.append(''.join(cur)); cur=[]
        else: cur.append(c)
        i+=1
    parts.append(''.join(cur)); return parts
def unescape(t):
    for a,b in [('\\<','<'),('\\>','>'),('\\{','{'),('\\}','}'),('\\|','|')]: t=t.replace(a,b)
    return t
def parse_record(label):
    if not (label.startswith('{') and label.endswith('}')): return None
    o=[]
    for g in split_top(label[1:-1]):
        g=g.strip()
        cs=[]
        for c in split_top(g[1:-1]):
            m=re.match(r'<n\d+> (.*)$', c, re.S)
            if not m: return None
            cs.append(m.group(1))
        o.append(cs)
    return o
def dewrap(cell):
    t=cell.replace(' ','')
    t=t.replace(',\\l', ', '); t=t.replace('\\l)', ' )'); t=t.replace('\\l','')
    return unescape(t)
def flat_width_cell(cell):
    lost=len(re.findall(r',\\l',cell))+len(re.findall(r'\\l\)',cell))
    return len(unescape(cell.replace('\\l','').replace(' ','')))+lost

def find_width(flat, actual, lo=12, hi=90):
    out=subprocess.run([LAYOUT, flat, str(lo), str(hi)], capture_output=True, text=True).stdout
    hits=[]
    for line in out.splitlines():
        w,r=line.split('\t',1)
        if r==actual: hits.append(int(w))
    return hits

rows=[]
for f in sorted(glob.glob(DOTS+"/l_R_*.dot")):
    name=os.path.basename(f)[2:-4]  # R_K_P
    label=None
    for line in open(f):
        if 'shape="record"' in line:
            m=re.search(r'label="(.*)",fillcolor', line); label=m.group(1); break
    if not label: continue
    groups=parse_record(label)
    if not groups: continue
    concl=groups[-1]  # conclusion group
    # cells: <nK> body ; but parse_record stripped the port already
    bodies=concl
    flats=[flat_width_cell(b) for b in bodies]
    T=sum(flats)
    for k,b in enumerate(bodies):
        wraps = '\\l' in b
        tag = 'Big' if b.strip().startswith('Big') else ('Sib' if b.strip().startswith('Sib') else f'c{k}')
        if not wraps: continue
        flat=dewrap(b)
        ws=find_width(flat, b)
        sib_flat=flats[1-k] if len(flats)==2 else None
        rows.append((name, tag, flats[k], sib_flat, T, ws))

print(f"{'name':12} {'cell':4} {'flat':>4} {'sibf':>4} {'T':>4}  Wrange   87-sib  87-T+flat")
for name,tag,flat,sibf,T,ws in rows:
    wr = f"{min(ws)}-{max(ws)}" if ws else "NONE"
    b1 = 87-sibf if sibf is not None else None
    b2 = 87-(T-flat)
    print(f"{name:12} {tag:4} {flat:4d} {str(sibf):>4} {T:4d}  {wr:8} {str(b1):>6} {b2:>6}")

Round 8 complete. All deliverables met.

UNIT B round 8 — RESULT: replaced the closed-form fill with a faithful HughesPJ layout engine and cracked the record-cell feeding. Wrapping-cell corpus byte-exactness rose from ~44% to **81.1%**; the Wide-rule probe is byte-exact for ALL cells; GRAPHCLEAN_CORPUS round-trip stays **12022/12022**. All servers stopped, ports 3200-3299 clear.

THE TWO FINDINGS THAT DID IT
1. **Ribbon = 1.5.** The 87 flat-fit boundary is HughesPJ's *ribbon*, not the line length. Rendering at ribbonsPerLine=1.5 (lineLength = ⌊3·budget/2⌋, i.e. 1.5× the ribbon) makes `fill` produce RAGGED paragraph fills — a physical line can be shorter than a later one. That reproduces corpus cells like `St_1_gNB( ~gNB_ID, KD8,` │ `KD1, '0', AM2, GN1` │ `)` (2 args, then 4 wider args) that no greedy fill (lineLength==ribbon, my prior model) can ever emit. This was the dominant residual: the closed-form/greedy fill was structurally incapable of ragged output. Derived by finding such cells in the corpus (band=[0,0] = unreproducible at any single width) then live-re-probing with `layout_at` LINELEN/RIBBON.
2. **Per-group share = proportional.** Each prem/concl cell gets fit budget `B_i = max(round(87·flat_i/T), 20)`, T = Σ flats. Lone cell (T=flat) → 87. Best of every allocator on the full census (proportional 81.1% vs smallest-first 62.6%, flat-sum 59.3%, prop-ceil 79.9%, reserve-small 77.4%). Reproduces Wide byte-exact: Ack→21 (wraps), Big→57 (8 tuple elems), Out→9→20 (fits). The engine at the right budget is byte-exact, so single-cell wrapping cells (budget always 87) hit **94.7%** — the near-ceiling that proves the cell doc + engine are faithful.

RESIDUAL (honest, ~19% of wrapping cells): (a) ±1 `fits` boundary where proportional lands a column off the reference's own coupled per-cell fits (e.g. `In_S( 'D2', 'H2', spkDD )`: prop=24.2→24 keeps `spkDD` on line 0, reference breaks at ≤23); (b) cells wrapped on their UN-abbreviated width (abbreviations substituted post-layout — outside a crate that receives post-abbreviation text); (c) `++`-union / deep function-application internal breaks the fact/tuple grammar doesn't model. All need the reference's exact per-cell fits over the pre-abbreviation document.

- src/doclayout.rs — cell grammar (fact `fsep`, tuple `fcat`+nest-`>`, info `vcat`) fed through the engine; RIBBONS=1.5 and `budget_line_len(b)=3b/2`; `wrap_cell_dot(flat, budget)` renders ragged.
- src/generate.rs — `group_widths` now proportional (replacing smallest-first); wired through `build_record`/`group_cells`; RawRule path unchanged (shares the pipeline).
- src/render.rs — STRIPPED to the still-live content/escaping (Fact, render_info, escape_record); the closed-form fill (wrap_cell, paragraph_fill, cell_budget, layout_*) and its stale tests removed; `pub use render::wrap_cell` dropped from lib.rs.
